blurb:
This food hall right off the Marietta Square is perfect when your family can't agree on what to eat. Everyone can get something different and still sit together. The setup is modern and clean, with communal tables that work well for groups or families with kids who want to wander around and look at all the options.

There are about twenty different vendors, so you've got everything from tacos to Asian bowls to barbecue all in one place. The variety changes somewhat, but there's always something good. My family usually ends up getting three different cuisines and sharing everything.

Parking can be tricky during busy weekend nights since it's right downtown, but it's worth the walk. Good for date night when you want options, or family dinner when nobody can make a decision.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu, Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
This is Brazilian all-you-can-eat done right, and it's become our go-to for celebrating birthdays or when we have out-of-town guests. The atmosphere is relaxed and friendly, not fancy, which makes it good for families. It's in a shopping center on Powers Ferry, and you'll know you're in the right place when you smell the grilled meat.

The barbecue is the star here, with servers bringing different cuts to your table until you tell them to stop. The cheese bread is addictive, and if you're there on the right day, try the feijoada stew. They also do breakfast, which is a nice surprise if you want something different on weekend mornings.

Come hungry because you'll definitely get your money's worth. It's perfect for special occasions or when you want to try something different without breaking the bank.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Sun-Thu 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This is perfect when your group can't agree on what to eat because there are twenty different food vendors all in one place. It's right by the Marietta Square, so you can walk around downtown after you eat or grab something before a concert or event. The atmosphere is casual and bustling, like a really good food court but with better options.

Everyone in our family finds something they like here, from pizza to Asian food to sandwiches. It's great for lunch meetings because you can each get what you want and sit together. The variety changes, so there's always something new to try.

Parking can be tricky during busy times downtown, but it's worth it for the convenience. Perfect for lunch dates, family dinners, or when you want to try a few different things without committing to one restaurant.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Sun-Thu 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
This is hands down the best pizza we've found in North Atlanta. The crust is thin and crispy like you'd get in Italy, not the thick stuff most places serve. It's become our regular Friday night spot when we don't feel like cooking.

They use ingredients imported from Italy mixed with local stuff, and you can taste the difference. The margherita is simple but perfect, and their salads are actually worth ordering too. Good for date night or bringing the kids.

It gets packed on weekends, so either go early or be prepared to wait. But trust me, it's worth it.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328

blurb:
This is our go-to when we're craving authentic Indian food that doesn't break the bank. It's in a strip mall near Perimeter Center, so don't expect fancy decor, but the food is the real deal. Great for families or when you want to order a bunch of different dishes to share.

The biryani is what they're known for and it's legit. The portions are huge and come family-style, so bring your appetite or plan on leftovers. We always end up ordering way more than we can eat because everything sounds good.

Just know they stay busy on weekends, so either go early or be prepared to wait a bit during peak dinner hours.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346

blurb:
If you've never done Brazilian all-you-can-eat barbecue, this is a great place to try it without the downtown Atlanta prices. Servers walk around with skewers of different meats and slice them right at your table. It's perfect for celebrating something or when you want to eat way too much meat with friends who also want to eat way too much meat.

The cheese bread that comes out first is addictive, so pace yourself because the real show is the barbecue. The picanha and lamb are usually the best cuts they bring around. They also have a salad bar with Brazilian sides like feijoada if you want to try traditional stew, though let's be honest, you're here for the meat parade.

Come hungry and wear stretchy pants. It's more expensive than your usual dinner out, but you definitely get your money's worth if you're a meat person.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu, Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This is where we go when we want really good Middle Eastern food without spending a fortune. It's tucked into a strip mall on Franklin Gateway, but don't let that fool you. The food is legit and the prices are great for the amount of food you get.

The shawarma is what I order every time, and the falafel is crispy on the outside and fluffy inside like it should be. They're good about making things vegetarian-friendly if you need that. The portions are generous, so it's perfect for a casual lunch or dinner when you don't want to cook.

It's super laid back and good for families or just grabbing takeout on your way home. Open every day until 9pm, which is handy for weeknight dinners.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This is the real deal for French pastries and bread in Marietta. The owner is actually French, and you can taste the difference in everything from the croissants to the quiches. The space is bright and welcoming, perfect for meeting a friend for coffee or treating yourself to a weekend brunch. I've started picking up their baguettes for dinner parties because they're that good.

The almond croissants are incredible and sell out fast on weekends. Their quiche Lorraine is what I usually get for brunch, along with one of those perfect little fruit tarts for later. Everything is made fresh, and you can watch them working in the kitchen behind the counter.

Get there early on Saturdays if you want the best selection of pastries. They close at 3:30 on Sundays, so don't plan a late Sunday brunch here. The petits fours make great gifts if you need to bring something to a party.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
This is our go-to when we want real Indian food that doesn't cost a fortune. It's in a strip mall near Perimeter Mall, and the inside is bright and colorful with that family restaurant feel. Perfect for when you want something flavorful but not too fancy.

The biryani is what they're known for, and it's legit. Big portions that easily feed two people, with perfectly spiced rice and tender meat. They serve everything family-style, which is great if you're coming with a group and want to try a few different things.

Just know they break between lunch and dinner on weekdays, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. Weekend hours are longer, which is nice for a lazy Saturday lunch.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
This is the kind of place that saves your weekend when nothing else is open. It's a classic diner with that neon sign on Cobb Parkway, open 24/7, which has been a lifesaver more times than I can count. Good for late-night cravings, early breakfast before a flight, or when you want comfort food without the fuss.

The menu is huge and covers everything from Greek specialties to big American breakfasts. I usually go for the breakfast even if it's 2pm because their pancakes are solid and the portions are generous. My kids love it because they can get breakfast for dinner, and honestly, so do I.

Just know the parking lot can get crowded on weekends, and service can be a bit slow during peak hours. But when you need food at any hour of the day or night, this place delivers.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is officially on my list of top dinner places in Atlanta. The vibe is upscale but not stuffy, perfect for girls' night out or date night when you want to try something different. Persian food isn't everywhere around here, so this feels like a real find.

The hummus and chicken shish kabobs are what everyone raves about, and the rice with lentils and raisins is way better than it sounds. Their cocktails are really well made, and save room for the tiramisu because it's worth the extra calories.

They have that weird break between lunch and dinner service, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. Make a reservation for weekend dinners because word is definitely out about this place.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328

blurb:
This is officially on my list of top 5 dinner places in Atlanta, and I'm not exaggerating. It's in the new Aria development, and the atmosphere is perfect for date night or girls' night out. If you've never had Persian food or think you don't like it, this place will change your mind.

Start with the hummus and definitely get the wings, which sound weird for a Persian place but trust me on this. The chicken shish kabobs with rice with lentils and raisins is what I order every time. The cocktails are all worth trying, and save room for the tiramisu because it's the perfect end to the meal.

Just know they break between lunch and dinner, so plan accordingly. Weekend dinner service goes a bit later, which is nice when you want to make a proper evening of it.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is where we take our parents when they visit or celebrate anniversaries. It sits right on the Chattahoochee River, and if you get a window table at sunset, you'll understand why people drive from all over Atlanta to eat here. Perfect for special occasions when you want to impress someone.

The seafood is what they do best, and you can taste the difference in quality. The atmosphere is intimate without being stuffy, and the service actually knows what they're doing. It's pricey, but you get what you pay for.

Make a reservation, especially for weekend dinners. And if it's a special occasion, mention it when you book. They do a nice job with celebrations.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339

blurb:
This place absolutely lives up to all the hype you've probably heard about it. It's in Sandy Springs Place, and from the moment you walk in, the vibe is vibrant and fun. Perfect for girls' night out, casual dates, or when you want tacos that actually taste like something special.

Start with the Esquite because it's packed with flavor and sets the tone for the whole meal. The Pineapple Guava pitcher is what everyone talks about, and for good reason. It's refreshing and perfectly balanced, not too sweet or too strong. The passion fruit one is great too if you want to try both.

They're open until midnight most nights, which is clutch when you want good food late. Service is incredibly attentive, and the whole experience just feels like a celebration.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This little spot near Franklin Gateway has become my go-to for Middle Eastern food when I want something quick but way better than chain restaurants. The counter service keeps things casual, and the portions are generous for the price. Good for lunch meetings or grabbing dinner when you want something healthy but filling.

The shawarma is what I usually order, and they pile it high with fresh vegetables and that creamy garlic sauce. My kids surprisingly love the falafel, which comes out crispy on the outside. The hummus is smooth and comes with warm pita that's clearly made in-house.

They've got plenty of vegetarian options if that's your thing, and the baklava at the counter is worth grabbing for dessert. It's in a shopping center but don't let that fool you about the quality.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This is hands down the best French bakery in the area, and it feels like a little piece of France right on South Marietta Parkway. The atmosphere is bright and welcoming, perfect for weekend brunch with friends or grabbing fresh bread for dinner parties. It's become our Saturday morning ritual.

The baguettes are crispy on the outside and soft inside, just like they should be. Their quiches are perfect for lunch, and the petits fours are beautiful if you need dessert for a special occasion. Everything tastes authentic, which makes sense because the owners are actually French.

Get there early on weekends because the popular items sell out. They close at 3:30 on Sundays, so don't wait too late if you want brunch. Perfect for special breakfast dates or when you want to impress guests with real French pastries.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
When you want to impress someone or celebrate something special, this is the place. Right on the Chattahoochee River with floor-to-ceiling windows and that upscale but not stuffy vibe. Perfect for anniversaries, business dinners, or when the in-laws are visiting and you need to step up your game.

The seafood is what they do best, with Southern touches that actually make sense. The crab cakes are what I always order, and the river view makes everything taste better. My husband usually gets whatever fish special they're running, and we've never been disappointed.

It's definitely not cheap, but the service is spot-on and the Sunday brunch is worth planning ahead for. Just make a reservation because this place fills up, especially on weekends.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
Finally, someone doing pizza right in Sandy Springs. It's in City Springs, so you can walk around after dinner, and the thin crust actually tastes like it came from Italy. This is where we take the kids when we want pizza but don't want to settle for chain restaurant quality.

The crust is what sets it apart. Thin, crispy, with those perfect charred spots that let you know they're using a real oven. They use ingredients imported from Italy mixed with local stuff, and you can taste the difference. The margherita is simple and perfect, but my kids love the pepperoni.

It can get busy on weekend nights, but the turnover is pretty quick since it's not a place you linger for hours. Great for a casual family dinner or date night when you want good food without the fuss.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This place absolutely lives up to all the hype you've probably heard. The atmosphere is vibrant and fun, perfect for a night out with friends or a casual dinner when you want something that actually tastes fresh. They're open until midnight most nights, which is a lifesaver.

Start with the Esquite if you've never had it. Their cocktail pitchers are dangerous in the best way, especially the Pineapple Guava and Passion Fruit ones. The service is surprisingly good for how busy they always are.

Expect a wait on weekends, but it moves pretty fast. And yes, it's worth the drive from wherever you are in North Atlanta.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328

blurb:
This is the kind of old-school diner where you can get breakfast at midnight or pancakes at 3pm, and nobody bats an eye. The neon sign is visible from Cobb Parkway, and inside it feels like stepping back in time with booths and that classic diner energy. Perfect for when you need comfort food at weird hours or want to take the kids somewhere with a menu that goes on forever.

The Greek specialties are what sets this apart from regular diners. Their gyro platter is huge and the moussaka is surprisingly good for a place that also serves burgers and milkshakes. The breakfast menu alone could feed a small army, and the portions are generous enough that you'll probably take half home.

Being open 24/7 makes this a lifesaver for shift workers or anyone with teenagers who get hungry at odd hours. Just know the parking lot can get busy during weekend breakfast rushes.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
